mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0% signals a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 8.0%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns weakens (Pearson < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production and GDP growth signals robust economic expansion, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or the interaction effect collapses during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 750,000 units signals strong consumer demand and confidence, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 750.0, or the predictive power of HOUST decays (rolling R-squared < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 2.5% signals rising inflation, prompting central bank tightening and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the inflation-SPX correlation reverses (Pearson > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production above its 12-month moving average indicates strong manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or the relationship breaks during supply shocks (e.g., oil price spikes).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising FEDFUNDS and stable CPIAUCSL signals a monetary tightening cycle with controlled inflation, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or the signal reverses during stagflationary conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.04) or the relationship breaks during liquidity crises.
